title: SDE→implementer handoff record (design-block)
description: Single JSONL record appended to .scratch/handoff.jsonl by system-design-expert after design validation, or after a build-failure escalation. Carries the architectural guidance that feature-implementer needs to write tests and code without re-deriving package placement, integration points, or design risks. Conservative required-field set; grow on observed retry deltas.

type.description: Discriminator. Always "design-block" for this record type.

req_id.description: Requirement ID linking this design back to the prd-entry.

ts.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the record was appended.

author.description: Pinned to SDE for this record type.

verdict.description: Routing signal for the coordinator. approved → implementer; needs_changes/blocked → bounce to PRE/SDE; revised → implementer (resets retry counter after a build-failure escalation); escalated → human decides.

architectural_fit.description: How the feature integrates with the existing design. Plain prose; references docs/system-design.md sections where relevant.

primary_paths.description: Paths the feature primarily lives in. At least one. Implementer reads this as the starting target set.

supporting_paths.description: Other paths touched (helpers, fixtures, related tests). Optional.

integration_points.description: Component-to-component connections introduced or changed. One entry per integration. Optional but recommended when the feature crosses module boundaries.

patterns.description: Existing code patterns to follow. Each entry references a file:line and explains the pattern.

risks.description: Identified design risks and their mitigations. Optional.

escalations.description: Items requiring human decision. Required when verdict is "escalated".

supersedes_record_at.description: When verdict is "revised", the line number (1-indexed) of the prior design-block record this revision supersedes. Append-only is preserved; this is a back-reference, not an edit.

notes.description: Free-form context that doesn't fit other fields. Avoid as a dumping ground.
